documentation.value: Outputs a message to the console. Multiple arguments are joined with spaces.
Values to output. Objects are formatted with util.inspect.

signatures.label: setTimeout(callback: Function, delay?: number, ...args: any[]): number

documentation.value: Schedules a function to be called after the specified delay in milliseconds. Returns a timeout ID.
The function to execute after the delay.
Milliseconds to wait before executing. Defaults to 0.
Additional arguments passed to the callback.

signatures.label: setInterval(callback: Function, delay?: number, ...args: any[]): number

documentation.value: Repeatedly calls a function with a fixed time delay between each call. Returns an interval ID.
The function to execute repeatedly.
Milliseconds between each call.
Additional arguments passed to the callback.
Starts the process of fetching a resource from the network, returning a promise that resolves to a Response.
The URL string or Request object to fetch.
An object containing request settings: method, headers, body, mode, credentials, etc.

signatures.label: JSON.stringify(value: any, replacer?: Function | Array, space?: number | string): string

documentation.value: Converts a JavaScript value to a JSON string, optionally replacing values or adding indentation.
The value to convert to a JSON string.
A function that alters serialization behavior, or an array of property names to include.
Number of spaces or a string used for indentation.
Parses a JSON string, constructing the JavaScript value or object described by the string.
The JSON string to parse.
A function that transforms the results before returning.

signatures.label: Array.prototype.map(callback: (value: T, index: number, array: T[]) => U, thisArg?: any): U[]

documentation.value: Creates a new array populated with the results of calling the provided function on every element.
Function that produces an element of the new array, taking the current value, index, and array.
Value to use as 'this' when executing callback.

signatures.label: Array.prototype.filter(callback: (value: T, index: number, array: T[]) => boolean, thisArg?: any): T[]

documentation.value: Creates a new array with all elements that pass the test implemented by the provided function.
Function to test each element. Return true to keep the element.
Value to use as 'this' when executing callback.

signatures.label: Array.prototype.reduce(callback: (accumulator: U, currentValue: T, index: number, array: T[]) => U, initialValue?: U): U

documentation.value: Executes a reducer function on each element of the array, resulting in a single output value.
Reducer function taking accumulator, current value, current index, and source array.
Value to use as the first argument to the first call of the callback.
Returns a promise that resolves when all of the input promises have resolved, or rejects when any input promise rejects.
An iterable of promises to wait for.
Returns an array of a given object's own enumerable string-keyed property names.
The object whose enumerable own properties are to be returned.
Copies all enumerable own properties from one or more source objects to a target object. Returns the modified target.
The target object to copy to.
Source objects from which to copy properties.

signatures.label: String.prototype.split(separator: string | RegExp, limit?: number): string[]

documentation.value: Divides a string into an ordered list of substrings and returns them as an array.
The pattern describing where each split should occur.
Maximum number of substrings to return.
Returns the largest of zero or more numbers. Returns -Infinity if no arguments are provided.
Numbers to compare.
Parses a string argument and returns an integer of the specified radix (base).
The value to parse.
An integer between 2 and 36 specifying the base. Defaults to 10.
